books.title: YESTERYEAR
books.author: Caro Claire Burke
books.description: Natalie Heller Mills, a privileged tradwife social media influencer, wakes up to find she is living in the comparatively difficult reality of 1855.

books.title: WHISTLER
books.author: Ann Patchett
books.description: Decades after their time together, a woman reconnects with her stepfather and they look back at the directions their lives have taken.

books.title: THE CALAMITY CLUB
books.author: Kathryn Stockett
books.description: As things get tougher during the Depression, several women look for ways to improve their circumstances.

books.title: THE EXQUISITE TORMENT OF LOVING YOUR ENEMY
books.author: Brigitte Knightley
books.description: The second book in the Dearly Beloathed series. Boundaries weaken between an assassin and a healer.

books.title: LOVE YOU MORE
books.author: Emily Giffin
books.description: Shortly after becoming engaged, Billie receives a call from her first love that compels her to return to Wisconsin.

books.title: THE CORRESPONDENT
books.author: Virginia Evans
books.description: Letters from someone she used to know push Sybil Van Antwerp toward revisiting her past and finding a way to forgive.

books.title: CARL'S DOOMSDAY SCENARIO
books.author: Matt Dinniman
books.description: The second book in the "Dungeon Crawler Carl" series. Carl and Donut confront an ancient spell and heightened dangers.

books.title: THE SHAMPOO EFFECT
books.author: Jenny Jackson
books.description: When Caroline Lash is pushed out of a social circle in a small seaside town, she exposes secrets that rile up the residents.

books.title: THE DUNGEON ANARCHIST'S COOKBOOK
books.author: Matt Dinniman
books.description: The third book in the "Dungeon Crawler Carl" series. Carl and Donut are up against a subway system filled with monsters.

books.title: DUNGEON CRAWLER CARL
books.author: Matt Dinniman
books.description: A Coast Guard vet named Carl and his ex-girlfriend’s cat, Princess Donut, are trapped in a fantasy dungeon.

books.title: COUNTRY PEOPLE
books.author: Daniel Mason
books.description: When his wife accepts a visiting professorship in Vermont, Miles encounters a story that may have more to it than seems at first glance.

books.title: HE WHO FIGHTS WITH MONSTERS, BOOK 1
books.author: Travis Deverell
books.description: A middle manager at an office supplies store becomes an interdimensional adventurer.

books.title: THE BUTCHER'S MASQUERADE
books.author: Matt Dinniman
books.description: The fifth book in the "Dungeon Crawler Carl" series. The action moves to the sixth floor, which is known as the Hunting Grounds.

books.title: THE GATE OF THE FERAL GODS
books.author: Matt Dinniman
books.description: The fourth book in the "Dungeon Crawler Carl" series. Obstacles abound on the fifth floor of the dungeon.

books.title: EVERYTHING WAS BEAUTIFUL AND NOTHING HURT
books.author: Ben Reeves
books.description: A man who gives comfort to those who are dying bonds with a midwife and her daughter.

books.title: REGIME CHANGE
books.author: Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan
books.description: Two White House correspondents for The New York Times delve into the first year of President Trump's second term.

books.title: REVOLUTION
books.author: Eric Metaxas
books.description: The author of “Martin Luther” and “Bonhoeffer” gives an account of the foundation of the United States of America.

books.title: CANCEL ME IF YOU CAN
books.author: Dave Portnoy
books.description: The founder of Barstool Sports describes how he grew his business from a four-page newspaper into the digital media company it is today.

books.title: STRANGERS
books.author: Belle Burden
books.description: Burden retraces her marriage of 20 years in search of clues to help shape her understanding about its demise and to find a way forward.

books.title: COMMUNION
books.author: JD Vance
books.description: The vice president and author of “Hillbilly Elegy” describes how he perceives his faith played a part in his work in public life.

books.title: THE LAND AND ITS PEOPLE
books.author: David Sedaris
books.description: Essays on the passage of time, complicated relationships and some unexpected delights life has to offer.

books.title: LONDON FALLING
books.author: Patrick Radden Keefe
books.description: The author of “Say Nothing” details the efforts by the parents of a 19-year-old Londoner to uncover the truth about his mysterious death and secret life.

books.title: FAMESICK
books.author: Lena Dunham
books.description: The author of “Not That Kind of Girl” evaluates the effects that pursuing her creative endeavors had on her.

books.title: THE ANXIOUS GENERATION
books.author: Jonathan Haidt
books.description: A co-author of “The Coddling of the American Mind” looks at the mental health impacts that a phone-based life has on children.

books.title: EVERYTHING IS TUBERCULOSIS
books.author: John Green
books.description: The author of “The Anthropocene Reviewed” chronicles the fight against the deadly infectious disease tuberculosis.

books.title: AMERICA, U.S.A.
books.author: Eddie S. Glaude Jr
books.description: The author of “Begin Again” delves into elements of America’s difficult and complicated past, and what they portend for our future.

books.title: ON COURAGE
books.author: Julia Angwin and Ami Fields-Meyer
books.description: The authors of the New Yorker essay “So You Want to Be a Dissident?” profile people who stood up to authoritarianism.

books.title: THE GREATEST SENTENCE EVER WRITTEN
books.author: Walter Isaacson
books.description: The historian and biographer examines the concepts of a statement found in the Declaration of Independence.

books.title: WHEN BREATH BECOMES AIR
books.author: Paul Kalanithi
books.description: A memoir by a physician who received a diagnosis of Stage IV lung cancer at the age of 36.

books.title: SUICIDAL EMPATHY
books.author: Gad Saad
books.description: The host of the podcast “The Saad Truth” argues that what he considers misguided compassion is having a negative impact on society.

books.title: RANSOM
books.author: Daniel Silva
books.description: The 26th book in the Gabriel Allon series. A search for a British billionaire’s missing wife turns up secrets and an old enemy.

books.title: THE COUNTRY ROAD MURDERS
books.author: James Patterson and Mike Lupica
books.description: A former football player returns to the town where his father was killed.

books.title: THE ROMANCE REVIVAL
books.author: Christina Lauren
books.description: A scientist uses a classified technology to bring her landscaper husband back to life but he does not remember her.

books.title: THE GOAL
books.author: Elle Kennedy
books.description: Pregnancy complicates the budding relationship between a college senior and a hockey player.

books.title: THE LEGACY
books.author: Elle Kennedy
books.description: The fifth book in the Off-Campus series. Four couples experience the ups and downs of life after graduating college.

books.title: THE DAY AFTER
books.author: Brian Tyler Cohen
books.description: The author of “Shameless” looks at the relationship Republicans and Democrats have to power and prescribes ways for it to be used after Donald Trump’s presidency.

books.title: PUSH THE WALL
books.author: Frank Miller
books.description: The comic book writer and artist chronicles his career from his time in New York in the 1970s to projects made in Hollywood over the decades.

books.title: THEY STOLE A CITY
books.author: Lauren Collins
books.description: A staff writer at The New Yorker depicts the 1898 massacre and coup in Wilmington, N.C., and its impact on four families.

books.title: CATCH THE DEVIL
books.author: Pamela Colloff
books.description: A staff writer at The New York Times Magazine and reporter at ProPublica gives an account of the damage caused by a con man in Florida.

books.title: CARELESS PEOPLE
books.author: Sarah Wynn-Williams
books.description: A former Facebook executive gives an account of policies and leadership at the social media platform.

books.title: THE RENOIR GIRLS
books.author: Catherine Ostler
books.description: The lives of three sisters, who were painted as children by Pierre-Auguste Renoir, go in different directions.

books.title: Rogue Lawyer
books.author: John Grisham
books.description: The attorney Sebastian Rudd is a “lone gunman” who hates injustice and the system and defends unpopular clients.

books.title: The Martian
books.author: Andy Weir
books.description: Abandoned by his crew, an astronaut embarks on a dogged quest to stay alive.

books.title: Cross Justice
books.author: James Patterson
books.description: Detective Alex Cross returns home, for the first time in 35 years, to help a cousin who has been accused of murder.

books.title: The Girl on the Train
books.author: Paula Hawkins
books.description: A psychological thriller set in the environs of London is full of complications and betrayals.

books.title: All the Light We Cannot See
books.author: Anthony Doerr
books.description: The lives of a blind French girl and a gadget-obsessed German boy before and during World War II.

books.title: Killing Reagan
books.author: Bill O'Reilly and Martin Dugard
books.description: The host of “The O’Reilly Factor” recounts the events surrounding the attempted assassination of President Reagan in 1981.

books.title: Thomas Jefferson and the Tripoli Pirates
books.author: Brian Kilmeade and Don Yaeger
books.description: The war against the Barbary pirates in 1801.

books.title: Humans of New York
books.author: Brandon Stanton
books.description: Photographs and interviews from the creator of the blog and the book “Humans of New York.”

books.title: Between the World and Me
books.author: Ta-Nehisi Coates
books.description: A meditation on race in America as well as a personal story by the Atlantic's national correspondent.

books.title: The Boys in the Boat
books.author: Daniel James Brown
books.description: The story of the American rowers who pursued gold at the 1936 Berlin Olympic Games; the basis of the film.

books.title: Fool Me Once
books.author: Harlan Coben
books.description: A retired Army helicopter pilot faces combat-related nightmares and mysteries concerning the deaths of her husband and sister.

books.title: Private Paris
books.author: James Patterson and Mark Sullivan
books.description: Jack Morgan, the head of the Private global investigative agency, probes the murders of members of the French cultural elite.

books.title: The Nest
books.author: Cynthia D'Aprix Sweeney
books.description: Siblings in a dysfunctional New York family must grapple with a reduced inheritance.

books.title: Me Before You
books.author: Jojo Moyes
books.description: A young woman who has barely been farther afield than her English village finds herself while caring for a wealthy, embittered quadriplegic.

books.title: Property of a Noblewoman
books.author: Danielle Steel
books.description: Two New Yorkers searching for the owner of an abandoned safe-deposit box reconstruct the history of a young woman and a forbidden love affair at the time of World War II.

books.title: Miracles From Heaven
books.author: Christy Beam
books.description: A mother tells the story of her once-sickly young daughter whose chronic disease disappeared after her near-death experience.

books.title: Dead Wake
books.author: Erik Larson
books.description: The last voyage of the Lusitania, the passenger liner sunk by a German torpedo in 1915; by the author of “The Devil in the White City.”

books.title: Frozen in Time
books.author: Mitchell Zuckoff
books.description: An account both of airmen’s fight for survival in the Arctic after a 1942 crash, and of a 2012 expedition to Greenland to find the remains of a plane that crashed in a rescue attempt.

books.title: End of Watch
books.author: Stephen King
books.description: The conclusion of the Bill Hodges trilogy.

books.title: The Pursuit
books.author: Janet Evanovich and Lee Goldberg
books.description: The F.B.I. agent Kate O’Hare and her con man partner, Nick Fox, face off against a dangerous ex-Serbian military officer.

books.title: Bay of Sighs
books.author: Nora Roberts
books.description: In this second installment of the Guardians trilogy, Sawyer brings the group to the island of Capri, where he’s drawn to the mermaid Annika.

books.title: Here'S to Us
books.author: Elin Hilderbrand
books.description: Sparks fly as a celebrity chef’s ex-wives pile into a small cabin in Nantucket to join his widow for the reading of his will.

books.title: Alexander Hamilton
books.author: Ron Chernow
books.description: First published in 2004, this biography of a founding father has been turned into the Broadway hip-hop musical "Hamilton."

books.title: Bill O'Reilly'S Legends and Lies
books.author: David Fisher
books.description: Stories of the American Revolution; a companion volume to the Fox News series.

books.title: Hamilton
books.author: Lin-Manuel Miranda and Jeremy McCarter
books.description: The libretto of the award-winning musical, with backstage photos, a production history and interviews with the cast.

books.title: Grit
books.author: Angela Duckworth
books.description: A psychologist says passion and perseverance are the keys to success.

books.title: Home
books.author: Harlan Coben
books.description: Myron Bolitar and his friend Win locate a boy who was kidnapped 10 years earlier; the 11th Myron Bolitar novel.

books.title: The Kept Woman
books.author: Karin Slaughter
books.description: Will Trent of the Georgia Bureau of Investigation and his lover, the medical examiner Sara Linton, pursue a case involving a dirty Atlanta cop.

books.title: Magic Binds
books.author: Ilona Andrews
books.description: The mercenary Kate Daniels and the man she loves decide to marry, but there are steep obstacles standing in the way to the altar.

books.title: Commonwealth
books.author: Ann Patchett
books.description: Five decades in the lives of two families remade by divorce.

books.title: Killing the Rising Sun
books.author: Bill O'Reilly and Martin Dugard
books.description: The host of “The O’Reilly Factor” recounts the final years of World War II.

books.title: Love Warrior
books.author: Glennon Doyle Melton
books.description: After her husband confesses to multiple infidelities, a woman who has overcome bulimia and alcoholism struggles to grow — and so does he.

books.title: The Girl With the Lower Back Tattoo
books.author: Amy Schumer
books.description: Humorous personal essays by the comedian, actor and writer.

books.title: Hillbilly Elegy
books.author: JD Vance
books.description: A Yale Law School graduate looks at the struggles of the white working class through the story of his own childhood in the Rust Belt.

books.title: Hero of the Empire
books.author: Candice Millard
books.description: The young Winston Churchill’s adventures in Africa during the Boer War, including an escape from captivity in 1899.

books.title: The Wrong Side of Goodbye
books.author: Michael Connelly
books.description: Harry Bosch helps track a serial rapist, while as a P.I. he aids a billionaire in search of a possible heir.

books.title: No Man'S Land
books.author: David Baldacci
books.description: John Puller, a special agent with the Army, searches for the truth about his mother, who disappeared 30 years ago.

books.title: The Whistler
books.author: John Grisham
books.description: A whistleblower alerts a Florida investigator to judicial corruption involving the Mob and Indian casinos.

books.title: A Dog'S Purpose
books.author: W. Bruce Cameron
books.description: A canine narrator undergoes a series of reincarnations.

books.title: Crash and Burn
books.author: Fern Michaels
books.description: In the Sisterhood's latest outing, the cohort faces a mudslinging Capitol Hill divorce.

books.title: The Princess Diarist
books.author: Carrie Fisher
books.description: Recollections of life on the set of the first Star Wars movie by the actress and writer, who died in December.

books.title: Wishful Drinking
books.author: Carrie Fisher
books.description: A memoir based on Fisher’s one-woman autobiographical show. Originally published in 2008.

books.title: The Undoing Project
books.author: Michael Lewis
books.description: How the psychologists Daniel Kahneman and Amos Tversky upended assumptions about the decision-making process and invented the field of behavioral economics.

books.title: Mississippi Blood
books.author: Greg Iles
books.description: Penn Cage, now the mayor of Natchez, Miss., investigates the secrets of his family and the South in order to save his and his father’s lives in the conclusion to the Natchez Burning trilogy.

books.title: The Shack
books.author: William P. Young
books.description: A man whose daughter was abducted is invited to an isolated shack, apparently by God.

books.title: Bound Together
books.author: Christine Feehan
books.description: Viktor returns to Sea Haven to see the wife whose stepfather he killed.

books.title: Vicious Circle
books.author: C. J. Box
books.description: Newly released from prison, Dallas Cates tries to exact revenge on the Wyoming game warden Joe Pickett, who put him there.

books.title: If Not for You
books.author: Debbie Macomber
books.description: An unlikely relationship is put to the test.

books.title: The Zookeeper’S Wife
books.author: Diane Ackerman
books.description: How a Warsaw couple sheltered Jews and members of the Resistance during World War II. Originally published in 2007; now the basis of a movie.

books.title: The Book of Joy
books.author: the Dalai Lama and Desmond Tutu with Douglas Abrams
books.description: A discussion between two spiritual leaders about how to find joy in the face of suffering.

books.title: Hidden Figures
books.author: Margot Lee Shetterly
books.description: he story, based in part on interviews, of the black women mathematicians who were hired as “computers” by the precursor of NASA during World War II. The basis of the movie.

books.title: A Colony in a Nation
books.author: Chris Hayes
books.description: The MSNC host discusses race relations with a focus on the differences in policing in the white nation and its black colony.

books.title: Camino Island
books.author: John Grisham
books.description: A search for stolen rare manuscripts leads to a Florida island.

books.title: Dangerous Minds
books.author: Janet Evanovich
books.description: Emerson Knight, an eccentric millionaire, and Riley Moon, a financial analyst, search for a missing island.

books.title: The Silent Corner
books.author: Dean Koontz
books.description: An F.B.I. agent investigates an alarming surge in suicides, including her husband's.

books.title: The Identicals
books.author: Elin Hilderbrand
books.description: Complications in the lives of identical twins who were raised by their divorced parents, one on Nantucket, one on Martha’s Vineyard.

books.title: The Handmaid'S Tale
books.author: Margaret Atwood
books.description: In the Republic of Gilead's dystopian future, men and women perform the services assigned to them.

books.title: Astrophysics for People in a Hurry
books.author: Neil deGrasse Tyson
books.description: A straightforward, easy-to-understand introduction to the laws that govern the universe.

books.title: Al Franken, Giant of the Senate
books.author: Al Franken
books.description: A memoir by the Democratic senator from Minnesota and former “Saturday Night Live” writer.

books.title: Understanding Trump
books.author: Newt Gingrich
books.description: The former House speaker explains the president’s philosophy and political agenda.

books.title: Killers of the Flower Moon
books.author: David Grann
books.description: The story of a murder spree in 1920s Oklahoma that targeted Osage Indians, whose lands contained oil. The fledgling F.B.I. intervened, ineffectively.

books.title: Sleeping Beauties
books.author: Stephen King and Owen King
books.description: Women who fall asleep become shrouded in mysterious cocoons while the men battle one another.

books.title: Don'T Let Go
books.author: Harlan Coben
books.description: Det. Napoleon Dumas investigates a murder and uncovers clues about personal events that happened 15 years ago.

books.title: It
books.author: Stephen King
books.description: The fears of seven teenagers are rekindled in their adult lives by the terrifying title character.

books.title: A Column of Fire
books.author: Ken Follett
books.description: A pair of lovers find themselves on opposite sides of a conflict while Queen Elizabeth fights to maintain her throne.

books.title: The Cuban Affair
books.author: Nelson DeMille
books.description: A Miami lawyer solicits an Army veteran to go on a secret mission to recover $60 million stashed away in Cuba.

books.title: Killing England
books.author: Bill O'Reilly and Martin Dugard
books.description: Major events and battles during the Revolutionary War are told from several perspectives.

books.title: What Happened
books.author: Hillary Clinton
books.description: An inside look at her campaign and how she recovered in its aftermath.

books.title: Unbelievable
books.author: Katy Tur
books.description: The NBC News correspondent describes her work covering the 2016 campaign of the Republican nominee for president and his behavior toward her.

books.title: Hit Refresh
books.author: Satya Nadella with Greg Shaw and Jill Tracie Nichols

books.title: The People Vs. Alex Cross
books.author: James Patterson
books.description: Detective Cross takes on a case even though he has been suspended from the department and taken to federal court to stand trial on murder charges.

books.title: Two Kinds of Truth
books.author: Michael Connelly
books.description: While he investigates the murder of two pharmacists, an old case comes back to haunt Harry Bosch.

books.title: Origin
books.author: Dan Brown
books.description: A symbology professor goes on a perilous quest with a beautiful museum director.

books.title: The Wanted
books.author: Robert Crais
books.description: A single mother hires Elvis Cole to investigate her teenage son who is on the run after a deadly crime spree.

books.title: Twisted
books.author: Helen Hardt
books.description: The eighth book in the Steel Brothers Saga series.

books.title: Grant
books.author: Ron Chernow
books.description: A biography of the Union general of the Civil War and two-term president of the United States.

books.title: The Last Black Unicorn
books.author: Tiffany Haddish
books.description: The comedian recounts growing up in South Central Los Angeles and finding success after a period of homelessness.

books.title: The Disappeared
books.author: C.J. Box
books.description: A Wyoming game warden teams up with his daughter to find a missing British businesswoman.

books.title: Red Alert
books.author: James Patterson and Marshall Karp
books.description: The fifth book in the NYPD Red series. Detectives Zach Jordan and Kylie MacDonald investigate the death of a documentary filmmaker and an explosion at a charity benefit.

books.title: Ready Player One
books.author: Ernest Cline
books.description: It’s 2044, life on a resource-depleted Earth has grown increasingly grim, and the key to a vast fortune is hidden in a virtual-reality world.

books.title: The Great Alone
books.author: Kristin Hannah
books.description: A former prisoner of war returns from Vietnam and moves his family to Alaska, where they face tough conditions.

books.title: This Is Me
books.author: Chrissy Metz
books.description: A star of “This Is Us” describes her journey toward self-acceptance.

books.title: A Brief History of Time
books.author: Stephen Hawking
books.description: The British cosmologist reviews efforts to create a unified theory of the universe; first published in 1988.

books.title: Russian Roulette
books.author: Michael Isikoff and David Corn
books.description: Details of the 2016 presidential election, with an emphasis on Russia's possible involvement.

books.title: The President Is Missing
books.author: James Patterson and Bill Clinton
books.description: President Jonathan Duncan takes on adversaries at home and abroad.

books.title: All We Ever Wanted
books.author: Emily Giffin
books.description: A scandal sends members of two Nashville families into chaos.

books.title: The Woman in the Window
books.author: A.J. Finn
books.description: A recluse who drinks heavily and takes prescription drugs may have witnessed a crime across from her Harlem townhouse.

books.title: The Perfect Couple
books.author: Elin Hilderbrand
books.description: A body is found in Nantucket Harbor hours before a picture-perfect wedding.

books.title: The Rooster Bar
books.author: John Grisham
books.description: Three students at a sleazy for-profit law school hope to expose the student-loan banker who runs it.

books.title: Kitchen Confidential
books.author: Anthony Bourdain
books.description: A memoir-exposé of the restaurant world. Originally published in 2000.

books.title: Calypso
books.author: David Sedaris
books.description: A collection of comedic stories on mortality, middle age and a beach house dubbed the Sea Section.

books.title: Educated
books.author: Tara Westover
books.description: The daughter of survivalists, who is kept out of school, educates herself enough to leave home for university.

books.title: Things That Matter
books.author: Charles Krauthammer
books.description: Three decades’ worth of essays from the conservative columnist.

books.title: The Soul of America
books.author: Jon Meacham
books.description: The present political climate is contextualized through the lens of difficult moments in American history.

books.title: Vince Flynn
books.author: Kyle Mills
books.description: When America’s power grid is shut down, Mitch Rapp goes after a cyber terrorist.

books.title: An Absolutely Remarkable Thing
books.author: Hank Green
books.description: After posting a video that goes viral, April May must deal with the pressures of becoming an internet sensation.

books.title: Transcription
books.author: Kate Atkinson
books.description: Ten years later, figures from a BBC radio producer’s past as an M15 recruit in 1940 confront her.

books.title: Crazy Rich Asians
books.author: Kevin Kwan
books.description: A New Yorker gets a surprise when she spends the summer with her boyfriend in Singapore.

books.title: Lethal White
books.author: Robert Galbraith
books.description: The fourth book in the Cormoran Strike series. Detectives Strike and Ellacott investigate a crime a young man may have witnessed as a child; by J. K. Rowling, writing pseudonymously.

books.title: Fear
books.author: Bob Woodward
books.description: Based on hours of interviews with sources, the Pulitzer Prize-winning journalist describes debates and decision-making within the Trump White House.

books.title: In Pieces
books.author: Sally Field
books.description: A memoir by the two-time Academy Award and three-time Emmy Award winner.

books.title: Long Road to Mercy
books.author: David Baldacci
books.description: Atlee Pine, an F.B.I. agent who is haunted by the childhood kidnapping of her twin sister, must choose between protecting her career or the United States' democracy.

books.title: The Reckoning
books.author: John Grisham
books.description: A decorated World War II veteran shoots and kills a pastor inside a Mississippi church.

books.title: Where the Crawdads Sing
books.author: Delia Owens
books.description: In a quiet town on the North Carolina coast in 1969, a young woman who survived alone in the marsh becomes a murder suspect.

books.title: The Tattooist of Auschwitz
books.author: Heather Morris
books.description: A concentration camp detainee tasked with permanently marking fellow prisoners falls in love with one of them.

books.title: Fire and Blood
books.author: George R.R. Martin
books.description: Set 300 years before the events of “A Game of Thrones,” this is the first volume of the two-part history of the Targaryens in Westeros.

books.title: Becoming
books.author: Michelle Obama
books.description: The former first lady describes her journey from the South Side of Chicago to the White House, and how she balanced work, family and her husband’s political ascent.

books.title: The Point of It All
books.author: Charles Krauthammer, edited by Daniel Krauthammer
books.description: A collection of essays, speeches and unpublished writings by the late conservative columnist.

books.title: Sapiens
books.author: Yuval Noah Harari
books.description: How Homo sapiens became Earth’s dominant species.

books.title: Killing the Ss
books.author: Bill O'Reilly and Martin Dugard
books.description: A look at the postwar manhunt for members of Hitler’s inner circle.

books.title: Supermarket
books.author: Bobby Hall
books.description: A depressed young man’s secrets are exposed when the grocery store where he works becomes a crime scene.

books.title: Tiamat'S Wrath
books.author: James S.A. Corey
books.description: The eighth book in the Expanse series.

books.title: Wild Card
books.author: Stuart Woods
books.description: The 49th book in the Stone Barrington series.

books.title: The Cornwalls Are Gone
books.author: James Patterson and Brendan DuBois
books.description: An Army intelligence officer must commit a crime or lose her kidnapped husband and daughter.

books.title: The Right Side of History
books.author: Ben Shapiro
books.description: The conservative political commentator reflects upon what he considers most impactful to Western civilization.

books.title: Doing Justice
books.author: Preet Bharara
books.description: The former U.S. Attorney for the Southern District of New York describes some of his career highlights and gives insights into our legal system.

books.title: Backlash
books.author: Brad Thor
books.description: Cut off from any support, Scot Harvath fights to get his revenge.

books.title: Lost and Found
books.author: Danielle Steel
books.description: A photographer embarks on a road trip to reconnect with three men she might have married.

books.title: Summer of '69
books.author: Elin Hilderbrand
books.description: The Levin family undergoes dramatic events with a son in Vietnam, a daughter in protests and dark secrets hiding beneath the surface.

books.title: The Pioneers
books.author: David McCullough
books.description: The Pulitzer Prize-winning historian tells the story of the settling of the Northwest Territory through five main characters.

books.title: Unfreedom of the Press
books.author: Mark R. Levin
books.description: The conservative commentator and radio host makes his case that the press is aligned with political ideology.

books.title: The Mueller Report
books.author: with related materials by The Washington Post
books.description: Redacted findings from the special counsel’s investigation into Russian interference in the 2016 presidential election and potential obstruction of justice by the president.

books.title: The Water Dancer
books.author: Ta-Nehisi Coates
books.description: A young man who was gifted with a mysterious power becomes part of a war between slavers and the enslaved.

books.title: The Institute
books.author: Stephen King
books.description: Children with special talents are abducted and sequestered in an institution where the sinister staff seeks to extract their gifts through harsh methods.

books.title: The Dutch House
books.author: Ann Patchett
books.description: A sibling relationship is impacted when the family goes from poverty to wealth and back again over the course of many decades.

books.title: Inside Out

books.description: The Hollywood star chronicles the rocky relationships, body image issues and public perceptions that affected her attempts to balance family and fame.

books.title: The United States of Trump

books.description: The conservative commentator weaves interviews and personal history to portray the power and influence of the 45th president.

books.title: Talking to Strangers

books.description: Famous examples of miscommunication serve as the backdrop to explain potential conflicts and misunderstandings.

books.title: Over the Top

books.title: Know My Name

books.description: A sexual assault victim reclaims her identity and challenges our culture and criminal justice system as they relate to this issue.

books.title: The Guardians

books.description: Cullen Post, a lawyer and Episcopal minister, antagonizes some ruthless killers when he takes on a wrongful conviction case.

books.title: The Last Wish

books.description: Linked stories follow the exploits of Geralt of Rivia, a monster-slaying mercenary.

books.title: The Night Fire

books.description: Harry Bosch and Renée Ballard return to take up a case that held the attention of Bosch’s mentor.

books.title: Me

books.title: Sam Houston and the Alamo Avengers

books.description: The "Fox & Friends” host gives an account of the battle against the Mexican Army in 1836.

books.title: Little Fires Everywhere

books.description: An artist with a mysterious past and a disregard for the status quo upends a quiet town outside Cleveland.

books.title: The Boy From the Woods

books.description: When a girl goes missing, a private investigator’s feral childhood becomes an asset in the search.

books.title: The Sinner
books.author: J.R. Ward
books.description: The 18th book in the Black Dagger Brotherhood series. Jo Early is attracted to a potentially dangerous stranger.

books.title: The Last Odyssey
books.author: James Rollins
books.description: The 15th book in the Sigma Force series. Catastrophic dangers might be set in motion when a medieval ship is discovered in Greenland.

books.title: The Splendid and the Vile
books.author: Erik Larson
books.description: An examination of the leadership of the prime minister Winston Churchill.

books.title: Untamed
books.author: Glennon Doyle
books.description: The activist and public speaker describes her journey of listening to her inner voice.

books.title: Lady in Waiting

books.description: A memoir that provides a look into the royal family by a lady-in-waiting to Princess Margaret.

books.title: The Great Influenza

books.description: An overview of the 1918 flu epidemic and cautionary tale for similar kinds of large-scale outbreaks.

books.title: 28 Summers

books.description: A relationship that started in 1993 between Mallory Blessing and Jake McCloud comes to light while she is on her deathbed and his wife runs for president.

books.title: The Vanishing Half

books.title: Camino Winds

books.title: White Fragility

books.title: How to Be an Antiracist
books.author: Ibram X. Kendi
books.description: A primer for creating a more just and equitable society through identifying and opposing racism.

books.title: Stamped From the Beginning

books.description: Winner of the 2016 National Book Award for nonfiction. A look at anti-Black racist ideas and their effect on the course of American history.

books.title: So You Want to Talk About Race
books.author: Ijeoma Oluo
books.description: A look at the contemporary racial landscape of the United States.

books.title: The Book of Two Ways
books.author: Jodi Picoult

books.title: The Coast-to-Coast Murders

books.title: The Evening and the Morning

books.title: Next to Last Stand

books.description: A collection of articles and speeches by the Supreme Court justice.

books.title: Blackout

books.description: The conservative commentator makes her case that Black Americans should part ways with the Democratic Party.

books.title: Killing Crazy Horse

books.description: The ninth book in the conservative commentator’s Killing series focuses on conflicts with Native Americans.

books.title: Caste

books.title: A Time for Mercy

books.title: Ready Player Two

books.description: In a sequel to “Ready Player One,” Wade Watts discovers a technological advancement and goes on a new quest.

books.title: Deadly Cross
books.author: James Patterson
books.description: The 28th book in the Alex Cross series. An investigation of a double homicide sends Alex Cross to Alabama.

books.title: The Return

books.title: A Promised Land

books.description: In the first volume of his presidential memoirs, Barack Obama offers personal reflections on his formative years and pivotal moments through his first term.

books.title: Greenlights

books.title: The Four Winds

books.description: As dust storms roll during the Great Depression, Elsa must choose between saving the family and farm or heading West.

books.title: Win

books.title: The Consequences of Fear

books.title: Double Jeopardy

books.description: The seventh book in the Fox and O’Hare series. Kate and Nick seek help from their fathers as they go after a shadowy international organization in search of a lost train full of Nazi gold.

books.title: The Code Breaker

books.description: How the Nobel Prize winner Jennifer Doudna and her colleagues invented CRISPR, a tool that can edit DNA.

books.title: The Body Keeps the Score

books.title: The Last Thing He Told Me

books.description: Hannah Hall discovers truths about her missing husband and bonds with his daughter from a previous relationship.

books.title: The President'S Daughter

books.title: Malibu Rising

books.description: An epic party has serious outcomes for four famous siblings.

books.title: People We Meet on Vacation

books.description: Opposites Poppy and Alex meet to vacation together one more time in hopes of saving their relationship.

books.title: Killing the Mob
books.author: Bill O'Reilly and Martin Dugard
books.description: The 10th book in the conservative commentator’s Killing series looks at organized crime in the United States during the 20th century.

books.title: The Bomber Mafia

books.description: A look at the key players and outcomes of precision bombing during World War II.

books.title: Apples Never Fall

books.description: The Delaney siblings suspect their father of causing the disappearance of their mother.

books.title: The Jailhouse Lawyer

books.description: A young lawyer winds up in an Alabama jail that remains mysteriously crowded.

books.title: Daughter of the Morning Star

books.description: The 17th book in the Longmire Mystery series. A player on the Lame Deer Lady Stars High School basketball team receives threats.

books.title: Harlem Shuffle
books.author: Colson Whitehead
books.description: Ray Carney, a family man who sells furniture on 125th Street, gets a new clientele made up of vicious and unsavory characters.

books.title: Peril
books.author: Bob Woodward and Robert Costa
books.description: The Washington Post journalists detail the dangers and challenges during the transition to the Biden presidency.

books.title: American Marxism
books.author: Mark R. Levin
books.description: The Fox News host gives his take on the Green New Deal, critical race theory and social activism.

books.title: Yours Cruelly, Elvira

books.title: The Judge'S List

books.description: The second book in the Whistler series. Investigator Lacy Stoltz goes after a serial killer and closes in on a sitting judge.

books.title: The Stranger in the Lifeboat

books.title: It Ends With Us

books.title: The Storyteller

books.title: The 1619 Project

books.description: A singer-songwriter goes to Nashville seeking stardom but is followed by her dark past.

books.title: The Recovery Agent

books.title: Verity

books.title: One Damn Thing After Another

books.title: Crying in H Mart

books.title: Ugly Love

books.title: Happy-Go-Lucky

books.title: Dreamland

books.description: Musicians from different backgrounds are attracted to each other and a mother flees with her son from an abusive husband.

books.title: Fairy Tale

books.title: I'M Glad My Mom Died

books.title: The Light We Carry

books.title: Countdown

books.title: The Housemaid

books.description: Troubles surface when a woman looking to make a fresh start takes a job in the home of the Winchesters.

books.description: The conservative commentator evaluates the legacies of American presidents.

books.title: The Backyard Bird Chronicles

books.title: Lethal Prey

books.description: The 35th book in the Prey series. True-crime bloggers complicate Lucas Davenport and Virgil Flowers’s search for a killer.

books.title: Onyx Storm

books.description: The third book in the Empyrean series. As enemies gain traction, Violet Sorrengail goes beyond the Aretian wards in search of allies.

books.title: Nobody'S Fool

books.description: The private investigator Sami Kierce tracks down clues to solve a mystery from his past.

books.title: Summer in the City

books.description: A screenwriter seeking inspiration coincidentally moves next door to the billionaire with whom she hooked up a couple years ago.

books.title: The Primal of Blood and Bone
books.author: Jennifer L. Armentrout
books.description: The sixth book in the Blood and Ash series. The Blood Crown has fallen and the Primal of Death must be stopped.

books.title: The Secret of Secrets
books.author: Dan Brown
books.description: As he searches for the missing noetic scientist he has been seeing, Robert Langdon discovers something regarding a secret project.

books.title: Circle of Days
books.author: Ken Follett
books.description: A priestess envisions a great stone circle put together by divided tribes, but drought and violence may impede its creation.

books.title: Tourist Season
books.author: Brynne Weaver
books.description: When a true crime investigator comes to Cape Carnage in search of a serial killer, a local gardener and a handsome tourist pause their deadly ways.

books.title: 107 Days
books.author: Kamala Harris
books.description: The former vice president recounts her abbreviated campaign to become president in 2024.

books.title: Poems & Prayers
books.author: Matthew McConaughey
books.description: The actor and author of “Greenlights” explores elements of belief and reason that make up our lives.

books.title: Awake
books.author: Jen Hatmaker
books.description: The host of the "For the Love" podcast describes major shifts in her life after her marriage of 26 years ended.

books.title: Confronting Evil
books.author: Bill O'Reilly and Josh Hammer
books.description: O'Reilly and Hammer profile some of history's nefarious characters.

books.title: Softly, as I Leave You
books.author: Priscilla Beaulieu Presley with Mary Jane Ross
books.description: Presley recounts her tribulations and search for identity after spending a decade with Elvis.

books.title: Theo of Golden
books.author: Allen Levi
books.description: A man travels to a small Southern town, where he purchases pencil drawings of local residents and exchanges them for stories.

books.title: Project Hail Mary
books.author: Andy Weir
books.description: Ryland Grace awakes from a long sleep alone and far from home, and the fate of humanity rests on his shoulders; the basis of the movie.

books.title: The Deal
books.author: Elle Kennedy
books.description: Hannah Wells makes a deal with the captain of the college hockey team in exchange for tutoring him that may complicate their relationship.

books.title: The Divorce
books.author: Freida McFadden
books.description: After her husband ends their marriage and she turns her attention to his new girlfriend, Naomi suspects she might be in danger.

books.title: A Marriage at Sea
books.author: Sophie Elmhirst
books.description: In 1972, an incident with a breaching whale turns a couple’s dream of sailing on a boat into a monthslong struggle for survival on a raft.
